defectsX.detail: format_cents does dollar math in binary floating point (cents / 100.0 then :.2f) instead of exact integer/decimal arithmetic — latent precision defect for very large magnitudes, and inconsistent with the parser's own Decimal-based rationale
Scope-creep stress test iterates parse_line over 200,001 amounts (range(-100000, 100001)), bloating suite runtime far beyond what the plan's parse spec asks for
CLI strips only '\n' (line.rstrip('\n')), so a CRLF-terminated file leaves a trailing '\r' on the amount field and every line fails with 'bad amount'
Comment/docstring noise restating the next line ('# Split on |', '# Read the file', '# Print error message to stderr') throughout — narration, not constraints

test_validate_edge_case_dates labels '2026-02-29' a 'leap year' check though 2026 is not a leap year and validate is pattern-only — misleading test intent (behavior asserted is still correct per spec)

defectsY.detail: test_validate covers whitespace-only description but never the empty string '' case the spec names first ('description.strip() is empty') — minor coverage gap

CLI strips the whole line (line.strip()) before parsing, so leading/trailing whitespace around a record (e.g. ' 2026-01-15|coffee|4.50 ') is silently accepted — slightly more lenient than the plan's DATE|DESCRIPTION|AMOUNT format implies
test_format_cents only asserts the three spec examples; no case for sub-dollar cents (e.g. 5 → '$0.05') to pin the :02d remainder padding

response.reason: Both meet the spec, but Y uses exact integer arithmetic end-to-end (X's format_cents reintroduces float math) and its tests are tight and spec-shaped, while X carries a 200k-iteration stress test, CRLF fragility in the CLI, and heavy comment noise — Y's gaps are only minor test-coverage nits.
